facility_size.rationale: loan_agreement.md §1.1: 'Lender commits to make available to Borrower a senior secured term loan facility in an aggregate principal amount of up to $15.0 million.' This is the actual committed loan facility amount, distinct from the §4.1 minimum-liquidity covenant threshold ($8.0 million) this field was previously (incorrectly) pointed at.
facility_size.provenance_note: §1.1 and this $15.0 million figure did not exist anywhere in the repository before 2026-07-22 — confirmed by grepping all docs, data files, the original mocked contract (data/contracts/sample_contract_response.json), and git history, none of which stated a facility principal amount. The field was previously mislabeled: its citation pointed at §4.1 (the liquidity covenant) and its value (8.0) was simply copied from that covenant's threshold, not from an actual facility-size figure. §1.1 was added as a new, explicit clause to loan_agreement.md specifically to give this field a real source to cite, per an explicit human instruction specifying the $15.0 million value. Anyone auditing this field should know the source document was authored to match this ground truth entry, not the reverse — the number is not independently corroborated outside this repository.
facility_size.caveat: n=1, single static value — this check can only ever score 1.0 or 0.0 for the one figure in the fixture, and cannot catch subtler errors (e.g. off-by-one unit mistakes) the way a multi-instance field could.

mac_style_interpretive_field.rationale: loan_agreement.md §4.5: the December 2026 pilot expansion is 'verbally approved but not countersigned, creating an ambiguous edge case requiring human review rather than automatic inclusion.' A verbal-only, uncountersigned revenue commitment is not contractually enforceable, so a credit analyst cannot treat it as committed MRR without sign-off — auto-including it would let an unexecuted side agreement affect a covenant breach determination. This judgment is derived directly from the contract text, independent of creditpulse.covenants.monitor_covenants' current implementation.
mac_style_interpretive_field.caveat: monitor_covenants() currently hardcodes human_review=True for every 'ambiguous' month with no alternative code path (creditpulse/covenants.py, committed_mrr_interpretation branch). With n=1, this field_accuracy row can therefore only ever score 1.0 or crash — it verifies that an ambiguous month is detected and escalated at all, not that a substantive interpretive judgment was correctly reasoned. If additional ambiguous scenarios are added to the fixtures, extend this answer key with an independently-derived judgment for each before extending the code, so the check stays a real test rather than a restatement of the implementation.
